options.text: the chronic need to imagine yourself otherwise, elsewhere, more

exercises.explanation: Named after Emma Bovary — the first user to confuse the platform with the world.

exercises.prompt: The three-stage psychological chain, in order:
exercises.explanation: The world closes over you; the avatar becomes a « je »; the elsewhere starts taxing the here.

exercises.prompt: What games do to the real/virtual boundary.
exercises.template: Le jeu {{b1}} la frontière entre le réel et le virtuel.
exercises.explanation: Brouiller la frontière — to blur the line. Repasser is ironing; leave the border unpressed.

exercises.prompt: The stage where the avatar stops being a pawn.
exercises.template: L'{{b1}} : le moment où l'avatar devient un « je ».
exercises.explanation: L'identification — stage two of the process. Indentation is for code; indemnisation is for insurers.

exercises.prompt: In narrative analysis, « qui voit ? » asks about…

options.text: the point of view — whose eyes filter the scene
the number of witnesses in the plot

exercises.explanation: Qui raconte (narrator) vs qui voit (focalisation) — Flaubert tells, but Emma often sees.

exercises.prompt: Logging off, as an act of will (verb, infinitive).
exercises.template: Se {{b1}} est devenu un acte de volonté.
exercises.explanation: Se déconnecter — the modern verb for surfacing.

exercises.prompt: Match each term to its meaning.

pairs.right: the grip on your real life

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order: "The novel was the first immersive technology."
Listen to the psychologist. Which stage is she describing?
exercises.transcript: Quand les amis réels attendent et que la guilde passe d'abord, l'ailleurs a commencé à taxer l'ici.

options.text: L'emprise — the elsewhere is taxing the here
La déconnexion — logging off for good

exercises.explanation: The grip is measured by what the real world pays: waiting friends, skipped meals, the third stage.

exercises.prompt: Read this analysis of a Bovary-style passage and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Dans la scène, Emma lit ; le narrateur glisse dans sa rêverie — bals, Paris, passions à la mesure des romans — puis une phrase sèche la ramène : la soupe refroidit. Qui raconte ? Flaubert. Qui voit ? Emma, tant que dure le rêve. L'ironie n'est pas dans un commentaire : elle loge dans le montage, dans l'écart entre les lustres imaginés et la cuillère réelle. Cent cinquante ans plus tard, remplacez les romans par un écran : le montage n'a pas pris une ride.
exercises.passageEn: In the scene, Emma reads; the narrator slides into her reverie — balls, Paris, passions sized to fit novels — then one dry sentence brings her back: the soup is getting cold. Who tells? Flaubert. Who sees? Emma, as long as the dream lasts. The irony isn't in a comment: it lives in the editing, in the gap between the imagined chandeliers and the actual spoon. A hundred and fifty years later, swap the novels for a screen: the montage hasn't aged a day.

questions.prompt: Where does the passage locate Flaubert's irony?

options.text: In the montage — the gap between imagined chandeliers and the actual spoon
In explicit mocking comments by the narrator

questions.prompt: What does « le montage n'a pas pris une ride » claim?

options.text: The same dream-vs-real mechanism works unchanged with today's screens

exercises.prompt: « Le réseau social promet le lien et vend l'attention » — the sentence's edge is that…

options.text: what's promised to you and what's sold by the platform are not the same thing
social networks are free of charge

exercises.explanation: The economy of virtual worlds in one antithesis: you are the link; your attention is the product.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Emma Bovary scrollait déjà — dans des romans.

options.text: Emma Bovary was already scrolling — through novels.
Immersion, identification, grip: three stages, one process.
The novel was the first immersive technology.

exercises.prompt: What does « un monde virtuel » mean?
exercises.explanation: un monde virtuel = a virtual world.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.template: Son {{b1}} est plus courageux qu'elle — pour l'instant.
exercises.explanation: « Son avatar est plus courageux qu'elle — pour l'instant. » — an avatar.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “Her digital identity works while she sleeps.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Emma Bovary scrollait déjà — dans des romans.
exercises.targetEn: Emma Bovary was already scrolling — through novels.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Le roman fut la première technologie immersive.
exercises.targetEn: The novel was the first immersive technology.
